title: Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 is a proposed law that addresses how artificial intelligence is used to support elderly people. It may provide guidelines or regulations on the use of AI to improve the quality of life for older individuals.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy that aims to utilize artificial intelligence in the Small Business Administration. The specifics of its implementation and goals are not available but the title suggests it's focused on leveraging AI benefits for small businesses.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to impose a temporary halt on the construction of new artificial intelligence data centers. The purpose of this act is not specified, but based on the title, it suggests a need to regulate and potentially slow down the growth of AI data centers.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy document aims to amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020, requiring the establishment of a prize competition program for artificial intelligence. The program would be managed by the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a proposed law aimed at regulating and defending against the use of artificial intelligence. The title suggests it focuses on safety measures and defense strategies related to AI.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act aims to use artificial intelligence to monitor and track heat-related emergencies. The policy likely aims to develop a system to provide early warnings and response strategies to mitigate heat-related health risks and emergencies.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.
summary: This policy aims to establish a pilot program for U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports along the Arizona border to test the use of artificial intelligence for anomaly detection. The program is intended to assess the effectiveness of AI in this context for other purposes.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act is a policy document aiming to support and empower small-scale artificial intelligence innovators and startups.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a proposed policy aimed at regulating and overseeing the creation and use of artificial intelligence. The details of the policy are uncertain, but its title suggests it may focus on developing, researching, and establishing guidelines for AI technology.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is a law aimed at preventing the misuse of AI in elections. It would establish regulations to ensure the integrity and transparency of election processes in the use of artificial intelligence.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy document outlines the governance and management of the Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence, regulating its actions and decisions. The Act likely aims to ensure the responsible development and use of artificial intelligence technologies within this department.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at addressing the environmental effects of artificial intelligence. This act would likely establish regulations to minimize the ecological consequences of AI's growing presence in various industries.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The American Leadership in AI Act is a proposed policy aimed at promoting and advancing artificial intelligence in the United States. It likely aims to increase U.S. competitiveness in the global AI market by providing guidelines and support for AI development and research.

title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act is a plan to require companies to disclose when their products or services use artificial intelligence. The goal is to inform and educate consumers about AI used in their purchases.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This policy is an act aimed at protecting families from the rising energy costs associated with the use of AI in data centers. The goal is to mitigate the financial burden these costs have on families.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This policy document deals with the regulation of artificial intelligence systems and their environmental effects. The "Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026" likely focuses on mitigating the environmental consequences caused by AI development and deployment.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating artificial intelligence across various sectors. It likely establishes guidelines and rules to ensure the safe, fair, and responsible development and use of AI technologies.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 aims to hold companies accountable for the fairness and transparency of their algorithms used to make decisions that affect people's lives. The act likely seeks to establish guidelines and regulations for algorithmic systems to ensure they are not discriminatory or biased.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 aims to regulate and manage the risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence. This act likely establishes policies and guidelines to oversee AI development to ensure public safety and security.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote and regulate the use of artificial intelligence in the financial sector. However, more information is needed to provide a detailed summary.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is a policy document that likely focuses on China's goals and strategies for advancing artificial intelligence technology within the country. This policy aims to strengthen and develop China's AI capabilities to increase its global influence and competitiveness in the field.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy that aims to set guidelines and regulations for the development and use of Artificial Intelligence models. The specifics of the policy are not available, but it likely addresses concerns around data protection, transparency, and accountability in AI decision-making.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy aimed at addressing the trust and confidence in Artificial Intelligence systems. The policy likely focuses on ensuring that AI workforce development aligns with public trust and safety standards.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This act aims to promote AI literacy and preparedness among K-12 students in schools across the United States. It likely outlines specific education and training requirements for students to be ready for an AI-driven future.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act aims to restrict the removal or alteration of content by AI systems, promoting free expression and speech. This act would likely set guidelines for AI algorithms to preserve user-generated content and prevent bias-driven censorship.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy, known as the Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act, likely assesses the risk of terrorism related to generative AI, a type of technology that creates content such as text, images, or audio. The policy's goal may be to identify and mitigate potential terrorist threats emerging from this rapidly advancing field.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This policy aims to stop certain companies from using automatic decision-making systems to manage their employees. The bill likely intends to regulate the use of AI in hiring and employment decisions.
